title: Bzorn
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Bzorn.jpg]] |name=Bzorn |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], [[Ionic Ring Ship]] over [[Little Kessel]]"[[The Second Kessel Run]]" |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin=[[Color/Legends|Light]] |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|With this ship, I'll have the Rebellion begging for mercy!"
"My invention was meant to stop suffering, not to create it!"
"You'll prevent your daughter's suffering, Volz... as long as you obey me!|Bzorn and Renn Volz|}} '''Bzorn''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as a [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] personally tasked Bzorn with devastating a series of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]]-held [[Planet/Legends|planets]]—he carried this out by commandeering the ''[[Ionic Ring Ship]]'', a weather control device developed by [[Professor/Legends|Professor]] [[Renn Volz]]. However, his plans were foiled by Volz's daughter [[Mira Volz|Mira]] and the Rebels [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], who stormed the ''Ionic Ring'' and started a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster fight]] that ended in Bzorn's [[Death/Legends|death]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|Your daughter is now my hostage, Professor Volz! An Imperial guarantee... that you will use your ship to turn Rebel planets into burnt-out wastelands!|Bzorn|}} A [[Human/Legends|male]], Bzorn reached the rank of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. In the wake of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]—with the tide having decisively turned in the war with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]—the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] directly tasked Bzorn with hunting down and laying waste to Rebel-held [[Planet/Legends|planets]]. To this end, he tracked down [[Professor/Legends|professor]] and inventor [[Renn Volz]], a native of [[Little Kessel]] who had built a device known as the ''[[Ionic Ring Ship]]'' that could control a planet's weather from above. Bzorn and a crew of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] forcibly boarded the ''Ionic Ring'' above the planet [[Zerm]], and took Volz's daugher [[Mira Volz|Mira]] captive to ensure his compliance. Mira Volz wasn't in Bzorn's hands long, as she managed to slip out of Imperial custody during a transfer to a [[Prison/Legends|prison]] [[Starship/Legends|ship]] at a remote [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], coincidentally ending up with a crew of Rebels when she stowed away on [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo's]] ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'' during her escape. Although his leverage was gone, Bzorn continued to bluff and forced her father to use the ''Ionic Ring'' to [[Devastation of Rion|devastate]] the Rebel base of [[Rion/Legends|Rion]]. From there they moved on to Volz's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Little Kessel, the next world on Bzorn's list. Bzorn forced Volz to [[Devastation of Little Kessel|attack]] Little Kessel with [[Groundquake/Legends|groundquakes]] and cyclones, but he could only push the scientist so far—unwilling to destroy his home, Volz refused to go any further, saying he would accept all consequences to him and his daughter. However, Mira Volz and the Rebels had followed the ''Ionic Ring'' to Little Kessel, and Solo and his allies [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] bluffed their way aboard and stormed the control room. In the opening shots of a frenzied, close-quarters firefight, Bzorn was struck and [[Death/Legends|killed]] by a ricocheted [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] [[Laser/Legends|bolt]] from one of his own troopers, ending his Rebel hunt. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|There are millions of innocent creatures down there, also!"
"That can't be helped, Volz!|Renn Volz and Bzorn before the devastation of Rion|}} A ruthless Imperial officer with a driving hatred of the Rebellion, Bzorn carried out his missions without any regard for innocent lives. Bald and intimidating, Bzorn could be absolutely merciless when pursuing a task, and had no qualms with threatening the lives of any who stood in his way. ==Behind the scenes== Bzorn appeared in ''[[The Second Kessel Run]]'', a [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] story arc written by [[Russ Helm]] and released in [[1980]]. In the comic, Bzorn was illustrated by [[Russ Manning]]. Bzorn later received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', released in [[2009]].{{CSWECite|I|99|Bzorn, Captain}} ==Appearances== *"[[The Second Kessel Run]]" {{Reprint|The Second Kessel Run}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' *{{CSWECite|I|99|Bzorn, Captain}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Bzorn |nl=Bzorn }} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Navy captains of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
